entries.title: Threading the Needle: India’s Path Forward with China
entries.description: This paper argues that there are predominantly four imperatives guiding India’s approach to China, and they exist in an order of priority.

entries.title: Nuclear Weapons and the Future of American Power
entries.description: It seems likely that, no matter what, the power of the U.S. nuclear arsenal will face erosion, not least in the credibility of its commitments to defend allies and the political durability of those alliances.

entries.title: Ramzan Kadyrov: The Kremlin’s Messenger to the Islamic World
entries.description: The reported failing health of Ramzan Kadyrov leaves a strategic communications gap for a Kremlin speaking to the Islamic world.

entries.title: Reforming European Security: A Turkish Perspective
entries.description: The Alliance is being pulled toward a NATO 3.0 model by two structural drivers: the changing character of warfare, made visible by the war in Ukraine, and the redistribution of transatlantic burden-sharing as the United States gives greater priority to homeland security and the Indo-Pacific.

entries.title: NATO 3.0 to Emerge at Ankara Summit Amid Fragile Unity
entries.description: European allies and Canada will try to ease recurring tensions with President Trump by stepping up in a rebalanced alliance: NATO 3.0. But transatlantic unity remains fragile.

entries.title: Will American Power in Europe Run Out?
entries.description: American power is entrenched in Europe. Yet the depth of this relationship has become a source of unease in Europe and Europeans are working to reduce their exposure to the vicissitudes of U.S. politics wherever they can.

entries.title: When AI Agents Attack: Autonomous Cyber Operations and Europe’s Governance Gap
entries.description: Autonomous AI agents are increasingly prevalent in cyberspace. The EU needs a real-time monitoring strategy, to invest in AI defenses, and to reduce its strategic dependence on U.S. frontier models.

entries.title: Ukraine’s Logistics Targeting Raises Questions for Russia’s Rear Defences
entries.description: Ukraine’s use of drone warfare has serious implications for Russian lines of communication.

entries.title: Network and Structural Power: The Four Trend Lines Weakening U.S. Leverage
entries.description: Networks—from international payments platforms to key economic sectors—underlie many aspects of U.S. power. But they are suffering under an extractive approach to foreign policy.

entries.title: China’s Grey-Zone and Geoeconomic Coercion Below the Threshold of Conflict
entries.description: China’s ‘war by other means’ is there to see, if Europe looks past isolated incidents.

entries.title: Threats to Maritime Trade are Spreading to the Indo-Pacific
entries.description: The ‘Bashi Breakout’ – China’s maritime operation east of Taiwan – is a warning that disruption to maritime trade is spreading to the Indo-Pacific.

entries.title: The Future of Russian Sanctions After the Hormuz Crisis
entries.description: US–Iran peace negotiations have stumbled, but if normalcy returns to the Strait of Hormuz, the G7 must be ready to align again on pressuring Russia.

entries.title: Crime, Violence, and the Reshaping of Politics in the Americas
entries.description: Latin America is undergoing a political transformation driven as much by the perception of insecurity as by its reality.

entries.title: East Asia’s Re-Ordering Milieu After 1945: Beyond the San Francisco Myth
entries.description: East Asian order should not be conflated with U.S. strategy in Asia.

entries.title: The Costs of Moral Pretence: How Hypocrisy Undermines International Order
entries.description: When the United States proclaims moral superiority while acting inconsistently with those moral claims, it pays a measurable price in global cooperation and international legitimacy.

entries.title: The Democratisation and Diversification of War
entries.description: Reflections on the effects of technological change and normative retrenchment on the character of contemporary war.

entries.title: From Hormuz to the Maghreb: The Geopolitical Reach of a Gulf Crisis
entries.description: Morocco and Algeria, each in its own way, are having to navigate the global economic fallout of the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran.

entries.title: Demystifying the Nuclear Threshold
entries.description: The nuclear weapons threshold is increasingly important for proliferation strategy and policy. Policymakers should better understand the implications of the threshold phenomenon in the current international security environment and plausible strategies to deal with the growing challenge that it presents.

entries.title: Securing America’s Near Abroad: Recalibrating U.S. Policy Toward Haiti
entries.description: Helping to stabilize Haiti is in the United States’ national interest and can be done by making wise use of various foreign policy tools in addition to supporting international security forces.

entries.title: Egypt’s Military Landlord Economy and its Limitations
entries.description: The armed forces champion a form of capitalism that is generating revenue, but its reliance on rent faces diminishing returns, leaving the country with massive sunk costs and deferred returns, deepening dependency on external borrowing.

entries.title: 25 Years After 9/11. The Next Global Shock Could be Infinitely Worse
entries.description: The next global shock may be the use of nuclear weapons assisted by the more casual attitudes of less responsible world leaders and the lack of popular understanding and fear.

entries.title: The AI-Enhanced Land Carrier Battle Group: The Way Forward
entries.description: The historical evolution of naval aviation offers useful ideas for overcoming the attritional nature of current drone and AI-dominated land warfare

entries.title: Episode 124: After Ankara: First Reflections on the 2026 NATO Summit
entries.description: The 2026 NATO Summit convened at a moment of fragile unity for the alliance. What did it deliver? What did it leave unresolved?

entries.title: Thinking Beyond the Baltics for Deterring Russia
entries.description: A rebalancing of the NATO Alliance should not involve Europe replacing US capabilities on a like-for-like basis.

entries.title: What’s Left of NATO’s Asia Engagement?
entries.description: Shared interests among European and Asian middle powers, navigating a turbulent Sino-American unilateralism, sustain NATO’s Asian partnerships.

entries.title: Recording: FATF Greylisting and Financial Inclusion
entries.description: This webinar explores the relationship between FATF 'greylisting' and financial inclusion, drawing on the Centre for Finance and Security's recent research and policy discussions.

entries.title: Episode 22: Delivering National Security Strategies
entries.description: Former UN Ambassador and National Security Advisor Sir Mark Lyall Grant discusses the challenges facing delivery of Western security strategies.

entries.title: European Sycophancy Worked on Trump
entries.description: Nearly a year and a half after Europeans leaned into sucking up to Trump, the strategy has produced some benefits when it comes to Ukraine.

entries.title: How NATO Became Anchored in the Black Sea
entries.description: NATO countries Turkey, Romania, and Bulgaria are upgrading their fleets and deepening trilateral cooperation in the Black Sea

entries.title: NATO Planning for War with Russia – but has Moscow Left the Door Open?
entries.description: Moscow’s strategic communications cast Russia as a backseat passenger on a road to war in Europe – but war is not inevitable.

entries.title: Cheap Attack, Expensive Defence: NATO 5% Pledge Following Iran and Ukraine
entries.description: Iran and Ukraine show cheap mass defeats expensive platforms. Unless it buys deep, low-cost defence and civilian resilience, NATO can win every engagement and still lose wars.

entries.title: AI in Outer Space: Opportunities, Risks, & Governance Gap
entries.description: How is AI reshaping space security, creating governance challenges, and where does international diplomacy stand today?

entries.title: What Should We Take From Andrey Melnichenko’s Essay in the Economist?
entries.description: Andrey Melnichenko’s essay offers no answer to the fundamental question of how, under any kind of negotiated settlement, Europe can protect itself from the Russian ressentiment that is inevitable in all scenarios except for an outright victory for Putin.

entries.title: Europe from Scratch: Visions for a New European Order
entries.description: Several leaders have called for major EU reforms, but only modest changes have resulted. What if the European order really could be reimagined from zero today?

entries.title: What’s Driving the Trump Administration’s Push to Dismantle the ICC?
entries.description: The move is an escalation of its war on multilateralism.

entries.title: The UK's Defence Investment Plan Has a Lethal Drone Swarm Vision
entries.description: Drone swarming is often referenced as an important future capability, with little understanding of what is possible today, and how the tactic is enabled.

entries.title: The Algorithmic Deterrent
entries.description: Palantir’s manifesto and the sovereignty question for states without algorithmic independence.

entries.title: Taking the Pulse: Enough with the Annual NATO Summits, Already?
entries.description: Over the past ten years, NATO has held almost as many summits as it did during the entirety of the Cold War. Are they still useful, or is it time to stop holding annual meetings?

entries.title: Carnegie’s Summer Beach Reads
entries.description: Carnegie experts recommend the books that kept them turning pages—and learning along the way.

entries.title: Putin's Fuel Dilemma
entries.description: Ukraine's deep-strike campaign is putting pressure on Russia's energy infrastructure and forcing increasing political pain for Putin.

entries.title: RUSI Reflects: Andy Burnham's Geopolitical Priorities as New UK Prime Minister
entries.description: With Andy Burnham set to become the next UK prime minister, RUSI's new director of international security evaluates the new leader's most pressing geopolitical priorities.

entries.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham faces a challenging international context, with intractable problems, but also opportunities to lead and exert UK influence on world events.

entries.description: This year's lecture explores the significance of national security and defence skills, and the role of universities in educating the next generation of engineers and scientists.

entries.title: In the Trump Gaza Plan, Diplomatic Failure is Success
entries.description: By failing to address Palestinian representation, the current process is bound to lead to just another temporary arrangement.

entries.description: Fedorov's removal leaves critical issues unresolved between the Defence Ministry and General Staff.

entries.title: Why Russia Could Escalate Hybrid Warfare Against Europe
entries.description: Mounting economic strain and battlefield losses will not necessarily make the Kremlin less dangerous. They could instead push Moscow toward a more aggressive hybrid campaign designed to test NATO’s Eastern flank, exploit allied hesitation, and fracture European resolve.

entries.title: Building Mini-Refineries Will Not Solve Russia’s Gasoline Crisis
entries.description: Official discussions about mini oil refineries are unlikely to come to anything—but the mere fact they’re happening reveals the regime is failing to deliver a functioning economy.

entries.title: Is AI as Bad for the Environment as Everyone Thinks?
entries.description: That statistic about a bottle of water may not live up to scrutiny.

entries.title: Japan's Ambivalent Policy Towards Russia and Ukraine
entries.description: Prime Minister Takaichi’s equivocal stance on policy towards Russia has encouraged a revival in activity from those in Japan who seek reengagement with Moscow.

entries.title: Russia’s Selective Adaptation: Command, Control and the Limits of Learning in War
entries.description: The contrast between Russia’s tactical flexibility and operational rigidity is revealing of the insecurity in its executive function.

entries.description: Iran Update Special Report, July 22, 2026

entries.title: AI Strategies and the Informal Economy: Africa’s Job Creation Test
entries.description: African nations have implemented AI strategies that focus on their formal sectors, leaving the informal economy behind.

entries.title: What Do the Election Security Documents Say About Chinese Interference in U.S. Elections?
entries.description: The biggest takeaway from the declassified intelligence isn't a cover-up—it's how intelligence analysis actually works. Healthy disagreement, documented dissent, and rigorous debate are signs of a strong intelligence community, not a broken one.

entries.title: Tracking Global Compulsory Licensing Trends in Biopharmaceuticals
entries.description: From HIV/AIDS to Covid-19, global health emergencies have revived calls for compulsory licensing. But history shows that innovation, technology transfer, and manufacturing capacity—not patent waivers alone—drive access to lifesaving medicines.

entries.title: Section 232 Explained
entries.description: In this week's column, Bill Reinsch explains how Section 232 national security investigations have become popular trade tool of the Trump administration, arguing that while some tariffs are difficult to justify, recent decisions suggest a more selective approach to their use.

entries.title: Charting a New Course: Countering China's Dominance in Global Shipbuilding
entries.description: China has developed the largest shipbuilding industry in recent history and now uses it as an instrument of national power. It is key to Beijing's ambitions to displace the United States as the preeminent power in the Asia-Pacific and project power far from China’s shores.

entries.title: States Weekly Newsletter: July 22, 2026
entries.description: This edition of States Weekly covers July 22, 2026.

entries.title: Where Are President Tokayev’s Political Reforms Leading Kazakhstan?
entries.description: Tokayev’s predecessor, Nursultan Nazarbayev, outlined arrangements for a power transition too soon and in too much detail, ultimately losing control over the process. Tokayev is determined not to meet the same fate.

entries.title: North Africa’s Flaring Problem is Europe’s Gas Opportunity
entries.description: The fallout from the Iran War has not been kind to European economies. A UK leadership change presents an opportunity to invest in combatting gas flaring in North Africa, so as to bolster security of supply, and tie ongoing international diplomatic efforts on Libya to that agenda.

entries.title: Losing Control of the West’s Longstanding Interests in Seychelles
entries.description: A democratic and well-functioning international financial centre that has benefited from Western support, Seychelles seems to now be choosing its friends elsewhere.

entries.description: Iran Update Special Report, July 23, 2026

entries.title: Will the U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Proceed?
entries.description: President Trump's last-minute link between the U.S.-Saudi nuclear agreement and Israel normalization injects new uncertainty into an already contentious deal.

entries.title: How the U.S. Can Survive a Large-Scale AI Cyberattack
entries.description: Advances in offensive AI cyber capabilities are threatening U.S. cybersecurity. The U.S. must improve its whole-of-society cyber readiness through enhancing its partnerships and communication before a major AI cyber-attack occurs.

entries.title: Detour Dividends: How Syrian Airspace Benefits from the Iran War
entries.description: This installment of Charting the Middle East spotlights the unlikely positive implications of flight disruptions for Syria in wake of the Iran war.

entries.title: India’s Stake in the Global Subsea Cable Race
entries.description: The Indian Ocean Region hosts one of the densest concentrations of subsea cables in the world. Yet, despite accounting for a significant share of global internet traffic, India’s participation in the ownership, routing, and maintenance of this network remains limited.

entries.title: Why Europe Must Take Charge of its Russia Diplomacy Now
entries.description: Trump is distracted and Ukraine wants Europe to step up. The continent’s leaders must find their voice and assert it in talks with Russia.

entries.title: A Competitive Analysis of the Biomanufacturing Strategic Landscape and Technology Stack
entries.description: Biomanufacturing—the ability to deploy biotechnology for manufacturing—is rapidly emerging as a global economic and national security priority.

entries.title: The Alternative für Deutschland and Germany’s Unsettled Place in the World
entries.description: Unresolved tensions within the AfD may ultimately constrain the its potential as a leader among European and global populist forces.

entries.title: Negotiating an Age of Conquest: Converting American Power Into Durable Political Outcomes
entries.description: There is a deeply rooted assumption in post–Cold War American statecraft that overwhelming military and economic power translates directly into favorable political outcomes. But force alone is rarely enough. To meet the challenges of the mid-21st century, Washington needs a cultural shift toward a mindset that takes negotiation far more seriously.

entries.title: A Path Forward on AI Safety for the United States and China
entries.description: “AI safety in parallel” is modest and pragmatic but vital.

entries.title: Burnham Has a Narrow Window to Shape UK AI Policy
entries.description: His challenge will come in balancing domestic priorities with a sharpening geopolitical environment.

entries.title: What We Know About the U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal
entries.description: Despite the agreement’s uncertainty, it’s still a significant development in regional and nuclear policy.
